actionEn: A broken line — crossing and changing lanes is allowed, only when safe.
descriptionEn: Marks traffic lanes; may also indicate a lane for slow vehicles, an ending lane, or an exit to another road.

actionEn: A solid line — crossing or driving on it is prohibited.
descriptionEn: Separates lanes of the same direction; crossing or driving on it is prohibited.

actionEn: A double line — only the driver on the broken-line side may cross it, prohibited from the oncoming side.
descriptionEn: Crossing is prohibited from one side, allowed from the other.

actionEn: Both lines are solid — crossing is prohibited from either side.
descriptionEn: Separates lanes of opposing directions; crossing is prohibited.

actionEn: Both lines are broken — crossing is allowed with care.
descriptionEn: Marks lanes with a variable direction of travel, controlled by a light or sign.

actionEn: The broken line becomes denser — warns of a solid line or danger ahead, do not begin overtaking.
descriptionEn: Separates lanes and warns of an approaching zone where crossing will be prohibited.

actionEn: A broken line at the road edge — you may pull onto the edge (e.g., to stop).
descriptionEn: Marks the edge of the carriageway.

actionEn: A solid line at the road edge — do not cross it.
descriptionEn: Marks the edge of the carriageway; driving onto the shoulder is prohibited.

actionEn: Only straight-ahead travel is marked on this lane.
descriptionEn: Indicates that only straight-ahead travel is allowed from this lane.

actionEn: Only a turn in the marked direction is allowed on this lane.
descriptionEn: Indicates that only turning is allowed from this lane.

actionEn: A U-turn direction is marked on this lane.
descriptionEn: Indicates that only a U-turn is allowed from this lane.

actionEn: The arrow shows the lane is ending soon and which way to merge — change lanes in advance.
descriptionEn: Requires merging into the indicated adjacent lane (the lane is ending).

actionEn: A "zebra crossing" is marked on the road — yield to pedestrians.
descriptionEn: A "zebra crossing" — the place where pedestrians cross the road.

actionEn: A crossing for cyclists is marked — pay attention to them.
The stop line before a STOP sign — stop exactly at this line.
descriptionEn: The stop line corresponding to the STOP sign.

actionEn: A line relating to a "give way" sign — yield to crossing traffic.
descriptionEn: A line corresponding to the "give way" sign (A-7).

actionEn: A stop line relating to traffic lights — stop at this line on a red signal.
descriptionEn: A conditional stop line before an intersection, crossing, or tram stop.

actionEn: A triangle on the road surface — indicates a "give way" requirement ahead.
descriptionEn: Road marking that duplicates the vertical "give way" sign (A-7).

actionEn: The word STOP marked on the road — a full stop is required.
descriptionEn: Duplicates sign B-20 "STOP".

actionEn: A line relating to a public transport stop — do not obstruct the stop area.
descriptionEn: Marks a public transport stop; parking is prohibited there.

actionEn: The line marks a parking place — park your car within the line.
descriptionEn: Marks a designated parking place.

actionEn: An access lane to a parking place — keep it clear.
An envelope-shaped marking on the road — do not enter this area even in traffic, keep it clear.
descriptionEn: A parking place reserved for a specific type of vehicle.

actionEn: A hatched section — entry or stopping here is prohibited.
descriptionEn: An area where entry and stopping are prohibited.

actionEn: The word BUS on the road — this lane is for buses only.
descriptionEn: Marking for a lane intended for public transport.

actionEn: A bicycle symbol marked on the path — this path is for cyclists.
descriptionEn: A bicycle path or lane.

actionEn: This parking place is reserved for holders of a disability permit.
Road marking indicating a speed bump — reduce speed.
descriptionEn: Indicates the presence of a speed bump on the road.

actionEn: The word "pedestrians" on the road indicates pedestrians may cross here.
descriptionEn: A lane marked for pedestrians.

actionEn: The marked path and arrow show the direction of cyclist travel.
